title: metrosideros polymorpha subsp. imbricata
intro: Metrosideros polymorpha subsp. imbricata, also known as the New Zealand rātā, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. Native to New Zealand, it has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to various health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Metrosideros polymorpha subsp. imbricata are typically used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
